# Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop

The EZ Pay Cashless Drop audit is required if the property has EZ Pay Smart Card or EZ Pay Mag Card functions available for players. These tasks involve determining and reviewing WAT (Wager Account Transfer) summaries for the current accounting period, balancing batches, and correcting variances.

The following tasks are available:

- **Import Cashless Information** — Imports system WAT-in and WAT-out revenue summaries by machine (from either Smart Card or Mag Card)
- **Audit Cashless Meters** — Audit and correct WAT-in and WAT-out variances between actual and meters

## 20.1 Retrieving EZ Pay Cashless Drops

The EZ Pay Cashless Drop is obtained from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance unless configured for drop-to-drop.

## 20.2 WAT-In Variances

WAT-in data is obtained from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance (unless configured drop-to-drop).

### Viewing WAT-In Meters

Data can be filtered to show All Machines or only Exceptions.

### Editing System Amounts

System WAT-in amounts and counts can be adjusted:

- **System Amount** — Corrected system amount
- **System Count** — Corrected system count

### Adjusting WAT-In Meter Variances

For each machine:

- **Value Meter Adjustment** — Enter the corrected value
- **Count Meter Adjustment** — Enter the corrected count

### Accepting WAT-In Variances

Variances within established guidelines can be accepted. Acceptance requires confirmation.

### Unaccepting WAT-In Variances

A previously accepted variance can be unaccepted for further investigation.

### WAT-In Comments

Adjustments can be documented with comments explaining the reason. Comments can be selected from a predefined list or entered as free text. Active comments are saved for reuse.

## 20.3 WAT-Out Variances

WAT-out data is obtained from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance.

### Viewing WAT-Out Meters

Data can be filtered to show All Machines or only Exceptions.

### Editing System Amounts

System WAT-out amounts and counts can be adjusted:

- **System Amount** — Corrected system amount
- **System Count** — Corrected system count

### Adjusting WAT-Out Meter Variances

For each machine:

- **Value Meter Adjustment** — Enter the corrected value
- **Count Meter Adjustment** — Enter the corrected count

### Accepting WAT-Out Variances

Variances within established guidelines can be accepted. Acceptance requires confirmation.

### Unaccepting WAT-Out Variances

A previously accepted variance can be unaccepted for further investigation.

### WAT-Out Comments

Adjustments can be documented with comments. Same comment management as WAT-In (predefined list, active/inactive status, Advanced configuration).
